extraction.one_line_summary: DAINESE Alberto won a photo-finish sprint after LEYSEN Senne's 192km solo breakaway was caught at 5km to go.
tactical_patterns: LEYSEN Senne held 2.5min max gap until 22km, then committed to solo from remnants at 5km—Breakaway riders willing suicide attacks late when group cohesion collapses.
MATTHEWS Michael launched early sprint from distance against pure sprinters, suggesting positioning trumps pure power when technical finishes limit leadout trains.
MILAN Jonathan blocked from joining breakaway by MATTHEWS who identified intermediate sprint threat—Sprinters policing breakaway composition based on secondary competition not just stage result.

rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: positive: Won home region sprint after full mountain stage workload, suggesting recovery resilience superior to pure sprinter rivals.
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: neutral: Nearly won sprint despite doing maglia ciclamino defense work, but teams now blocking his breakaway attempts for intermediate sprints.
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Competitive in bunch sprint against pure sprinters after long flat stage, launched early from technical position—classics engine transfers to Grand Tour sprints.
rider_signals.LEYSEN Senne: neutral: Committed 192km breakaway effort then solo from 5km with zero chance—Team prioritizing visibility over result optimization.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: 18-second GC lead maintained entering decisive mountain stages, team controlled finale positioning suggesting confidence not defense.
